practice_pattern.interpretation: Your code mix aligns well with regional peers for routine obstetric care; some gaps exist in gynecologic oncology referral codes and advanced ultrasound procedures

practice_pattern.payer_description: both Medicare and Medicaid claims data
practice_pattern.caveat_if_single_payer: Your payer mix is consistent with a full-service OB-GYN practice in a rural West Virginia community

practice_pattern.top_flag: higher-than-average rate of 99215 (complex office visits) relative to peers
practice_pattern.top_red_flag_detail: The top flag is a higher-than-average rate of 99215 (complex office visits) relative to peers in Princeton, WV — this may reflect documentation of complex obstetric patients but warrants review

credentials.board: American Board of Obstetrics and Gynecology (ABOG)

access.features_list: accepts new patients, evening hours on Tuesdays and Thursdays, 14 insurance plans accepted, Spanish language services available

improvement_path.action: Your billing has 2 flagged pattern(s). The most common flag is: higher-than-average rate of 99215 (complex office visits) relative to peers. Review these with your billing team — resolving flagged patterns improves your billing quality score.
Renew your board certification before it expires. Your current certification expires within 12 months; allowing it to lapse will drop your credentials score significantly.
Adding telehealth to your practice would improve your access score. Telehealth is one of the highest-weighted access factors. Even limited availability (e.g., follow-ups only) earns credit.

The following service categories are below minimum volume thresholds: Colposcopy, Hysteroscopy. If you perform these services, verify they are being billed under your NPI rather than a facility or group billing number.

You currently have 7 reviews, which is below the confidence threshold. Encouraging patients to leave reviews on Healthgrades or Zocdoc increases score confidence weighting — more reviews means your score carries more weight in the composite.

Only an indirect quality signal is available via your hospital affiliation. A direct MIPS score would carry more weight, but that requires reaching the Medicare volume threshold (~200 patients/year). This is a structural limitation for commercially-focused practices.
improvement_path._note: Included for completeness even though tier1 MIPS data exists for this provider in the explanation output — this recommendation would apply if the practice had tier2 data instead. Shown here as the structural non-actionable example.

quick_wins: Verify colposcopy and hysteroscopy are billed under your NPI (billing/volume fix, days to weeks)
Invite patients to leave reviews to raise confidence weighting (days to weeks)
moderate_horizon: Resolve 2 flagged billing patterns with your billing team (weeks to months)
Renew ABOG board certification before November 2026 expiration (months)
Add telehealth capability, even for follow-ups only (months)
structural_non_actionable: MIPS tier / hospital signal only — structural, no action required
